description: War fiction requires element configurations that place characters within armed conflict, emphasizing the human cost of violence, the tension between duty and survival, and the bonds formed under extreme pressure.

character_constraints.description: A soldier, officer, or civilian caught in armed conflict whose survival and moral choices drive the story.
An enemy force, opposing commander, or systemic military machinery — may be personified or abstract.
Fellow soldiers or comrades-in-arms whose bonds with the protagonist create emotional stakes.
A seasoned officer or veteran who provides guidance — may represent institutional authority for good or ill.

relationship_constraints.description: Bonds between comrades forged through shared danger — the unit relationship is central to war fiction.

place_constraints.description: The combat zone — battlefield, occupied territory, or theater of war where normal life is suspended.
A fortified position, base camp, or defensive line — represents temporary safety within the war zone.
Home, peacetime, or pre-war life that contrasts with the combat reality and represents what's worth fighting for.

object_constraints.description: Weapons and military equipment that define the nature of the conflict and the characters' capabilities.
A flag, letter from home, photograph, or token that represents what the characters are fighting for or what they've lost.

element_rules.description: The human cost of conflict must be depicted — at least one ally must die or be permanently harmed.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one 'dies' or 'loses' transition applies to an ally character during the timeline.

element_rules.description: The protagonist must face a moral dilemma created by the demands of war — duty vs. conscience, orders vs. compassion.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one 'decides' transition appears for the protagonist involving a conflict between duty and personal values.

element_rules.description: Combat must have consequences beyond the immediate battle — the war changes the characters permanently.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one 'transforms' transition applies to the protagonist, reflecting how war has changed them.
